key_insight: A hidden-target projection policy is provably optimal for online inventory optimization on general bounded convex capacity sets, yielding stronger regret guarantees by turning the state dynamics into a one-dimensional queue under norm alignment.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled online inventory controller for constrained systems with carryover, improving robustness and performance for operations such as replenishment, storage, and resource allocation under uncertainty.
method: Maintain an online-learned hidden target and execute its projection onto the current feasible order-up-to set.
Choose the projection norm to match the state metric, so the target-to-feasible-set distance evolves like a scalar queue.
Use online gradient descent as the base learner to obtain improved regret bounds, including strong-convexity and dynamic-regret guarantees.
evidence_signal: The paper reports synthetic and real-world inventory experiments that support the theory and shows matching lower bounds for the main regret rate.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on norm alignment and bounded convex capacity sets, so practical implementation must ensure the chosen projection norm and feasibility model match the real inventory dynamics.
paper_title: Optimal Hidden-Target Learning for Online Inventory Optimization on General Convex Sets
